channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Supreme Court: Film Failure Isn't Cheating Without Proven Intent
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that simply losing money on a film project isn't cheating unless fraud was intended from the start. The decision separates civil disagreements from criminal charges, protecting producers from unsupported claims. It changes how investor lawsuits are handled in film finance, requiring proof of intent for criminal cases.

item.title: India Telecom Subscriber Base Jumps 7.86M in January, Broadband Growth Lags
item.description: India's telecom sector gained 7.86 million subscribers in January, mainly from wireless users, bringing the total to 1.314 billion. Broadband growth was slow, but fixed wireless access (FWA) and 5G options are gaining early interest as competition heats up.

item.title: Jana SFB Profit Plunges 91% to ₹10 Cr Before Analyst Meet
item.description: Jana Small Finance Bank will host an investor and analyst meeting on March 24, 2026, to discuss its Q3 FY2025-26 results. The bank reported a sharp 91% year-on-year drop in net profit to ₹10 crore, mainly due to increased provisions and operating costs. Management will explain the performance and outlook.

item.title: India InvITs Boom: Growth Outpaces Bond Market Use
item.description: India's Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs) are set to grow their Assets Under Management (AUM) by ₹1 lakh crore by FY26, building on strong past growth. However, the sector remains concentrated in telecom and roads. A major issue is their continued reliance on bank loans, with little use of the bond market, missing chances for better funding.

item.title: Metals Tumble as Fed Signals Higher Rates, Oil Prices Soar
item.description: Gold and silver have experienced sharp declines, with gold futures hitting multi-day lows and silver futures seeing a significant drop. This broad commodity weakness extends to base metals like copper and aluminum. The sell-off is driven by the Federal Reserve's signal of higher-for-longer interest rates amid persistent inflation, coupled with oil prices surging past $100 and a strengthening US dollar, which are collectively diminishing the appeal of traditional safe-haven assets and inflation hedges.

item.title: Europe's Digital Euro Race: ECB Plans Clash with Bank Stablecoin
item.description: The European Central Bank is refining technical plans for a digital euro, eyeing a 2029 launch after 2026 legislative approval. Meanwhile, a group of twelve top European banks plans to debut a MiCA-compliant, euro-pegged stablecoin by late 2026. These moves show Europe's drive to boost the euro's digital presence and challenge US dollar dominance, highlighting a rapidly developing, competitive digital currency landscape.

item.title: India's Power Demand Surge: AI & EVs Strain Grid Infrastructure
item.description: India's power sector anticipates a summer peak demand of 270 GW, driven by AI, data centers, and electric vehicles. While supply readiness is assured, transmission infrastructure expansion is lagging renewable energy growth, sparking concerns over future grid bottlenecks. Companies like NTPC and Power Grid Corporation face investor scrutiny over rising costs and market shifts, even as foreign investors inject funds, betting on continued demand.

item.title: Bitcoin Prices: ETFs and Derivatives Now Set Pace Over Scarcity
item.description: Bitcoin's price is now determined more by derivatives and ETFs than simple supply and demand. New regulated futures and U.S. ETF approvals have linked Bitcoin to global markets, with institutional hedging strategies amplifying its price swings.

item.title: Fund Buys Rajputana Stainless IPO Debut; Bliss GVS Sees Investor Shift
item.description: Rajputana Stainless shares opened flat to a slight premium on March 19, 2026, closing down 7.35% at Rs 113.03, below its Rs 122 IPO price. Despite this, Craft Emerging Market Fund acquired a 2.57% stake for Rs 26.23 crore at the Rs 122 IPO price. Separately, in Bliss GVS Pharma, Mateus purchased an additional 0.9% stake, while Arian Investment divested 0.94%. Bliss GVS Pharma shares declined 3.82% on the same day.

item.title: India Secures Fertilizer Supply With Costly LNG Deal
item.description: India is boosting fertilizer availability for the Kharif 2026 season through higher domestic urea production and imports. The government is prioritizing natural gas for urea plants and has secured an expensive LNG spot deal. While fertilizer stocks look strong, the high cost of this gas raises concerns about long-term sustainability and potential price increases for farmers.

item.title: Geecee Ventures Fully Funds Bharti Airtel Stake with ₹9.27 Cr Payment
item.description: Geecee Ventures Limited has finalized its investment in Bharti Airtel, making a final ₹9.27 crore payment for 2,30,976 partly paid-up shares. These shares are now fully paid and carry the same rights as existing shares. This completes Geecee's participation in Bharti Airtel's 2021 rights issue.

item.title: India Launches ₹497 Cr Exporter Relief Scheme for West Asia Crisis
item.description: India's Commerce Ministry has launched a ₹497 crore 'RELIEF' scheme to help exporters cope with higher freight and insurance costs due to the West Asia conflict. The plan includes better insurance coverage, automatic extensions, and support for small businesses. However, this comes as India faces broader economic challenges, like volatile oil prices and possible shrinking demand, making its ability to counter major geopolitical risks uncertain.

item.title: Yokohama India's ₹1500 Cr Tire Expansion Risks Replacement Market Reliance
item.description: Yokohama India has doubled its annual tire production capacity to 4.5 million units with a ₹1,500 crore investment, aiming to tap into India's premiumization trend and growing SUV market. However, the company heavily relies on the replacement segment, which makes up about 95% of its sales. Its ambitious targets for Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) sales face tough competition. Additionally, volatile input costs, linked to crude oil derivatives, threaten profitability, especially when compared to the higher margins of specialized competitors.

item.title: HCL Technologies Fully Settles $500 Million Debt, Releases Guarantee
item.description: HCL Technologies has fully settled its $500 million in senior unsecured notes, issued by its US subsidiary HCL America Inc., with the final payment of $252.207 million completed on March 10, 2026. This repayment releases HCL Technologies' guarantee on the debt, signaling stronger financial health and disciplined management.

item.title: Iran Attacks Cripple Qatar LNG: Global Energy Prices Spike, Markets Shift
item.description: Iranian attacks on Qatar's liquefied natural gas (LNG) export facilities have crippled 17% of its capacity, causing an estimated $20 billion in annual revenue loss and potentially halting production for three to five years. The strikes, hitting two of Qatar's 14 LNG trains, have driven up global energy prices and intensified geopolitical risk in the region. QatarEnergy may declare force majeure on contracts to Asian and European buyers, impacting condensate, LPG, helium, naphtha, and sulfur exports as well.

item.title: ICICI Bank Faces ₹768 Crore GST Demand
item.description: ICICI Bank confirmed it received a Goods and Services Tax (GST) demand notice totaling ₹768.6 crore for alleged short payments on services related to minimum balance accounts. This includes ₹384.3 crore in demand, matched by an equal amount for penalty and interest. The bank intends to contest the order, citing ongoing litigation for similar past issues. This substantial demand highlights ongoing tax scrutiny that major financial institutions face.

item.title: India Retailers Discount Deeply Amid Soaring Costs, Geopolitical Fears
item.description: Indian retailers are offering deep discounts to sell off stock before expected price increases. These hikes are driven by surging memory chip costs, higher shipping fees, and a falling rupee. The conflict in the Gulf adds pressure, boosting oil prices and weakening the rupee, which could hit consumer spending. While the sector has long-term potential, current challenges are significant.

item.title: Amazon's India Content Push: Global Reach Faces Big Risks
item.description: India is now central to Amazon Prime Video's global content plans, with local shows gaining a significant international following – nearly a quarter of viewers are outside India. Amazon is investing heavily in creating franchises and expanding its movie business, using its vast global network to promote Indian content worldwide. The strategy faces tough competition in India's fast-growing streaming market from rivals like Disney+ Hotstar and Netflix, alongside regulatory hurdles and piracy risks.

item.title: Nestle India Invests ₹225 Crore to Boost Munch Production Amid Strong Demand
item.description: Nestle India is investing ₹225 crore to expand its Sanand factory's production capacity for its Munch chocolate brand by 8,300 tonnes per year. Funded internally, this move aims to meet growing demand in India's confectionery market, which is seeing strong growth. While recent Q3 FY26 results showed a 46% profit increase and good volume growth, margins were affected by higher advertising costs. The investment positions Nestle to benefit from changing consumer tastes and a market expected to keep expanding, despite increasing competition.

item.title: IREDA Board to Decide Interim Dividend March 25, Trading Window Closed
item.description: Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) plans a Board meeting on March 25, 2026, to consider an interim dividend for FY25-26. Its trading window will close until 48 hours after the meeting, halting trades for insiders.

item.title: IREDA Board Meets March 25 on FY26 Interim Dividend
item.description: Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) will hold a Board Meeting on March 25, 2026, to consider an interim dividend for FY26. The decision could impact shareholder returns. The company's trading window will be closed until 48 hours after the meeting.

item.title: JSW Cement Opens First North India Plant, Boosts Capacity
item.description: JSW Cement has opened its first integrated cement plant in Nagaur, Rajasthan, significantly expanding its reach into North India. The new facility adds 2.5 MTPA grinding capacity and 3.3 MTPA clinkerisation capacity, boosting overall production and strengthening its nationwide presence.

item.title: Google X's Anori Launches: Industry Investors Fund Construction Tech Startup
item.description: Google's X innovation lab has spun out Anori, a construction technology platform focused on the crucial pre-development phase. Anori secured $26 million in funding, co-led by Prologis and Builders VC. Its strategy involves making industry players investors, a key difference from past X projects. The company aims to simplify the lengthy process of getting building approvals and starting construction, tackling a major hurdle in housing and commercial projects.

item.title: Adani Power expresses interest in Jaiprakash Associates' power assets
item.description: Adani Power is looking to buy power assets from Jaiprakash Associates (JAL) through a bankruptcy process. This follows the NCLT's approval of JAL's plan, but the deal still needs further approvals.

item.title: Adani Ports Pursues JAL Assets as NCLT Approves Resolution Plan
item.description: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited (APSEZ) has expressed interest in becoming the entity to implement the resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates Limited (JAL). This follows the NCLT's approval of JAL's plan, with APSEZ aiming to acquire aviation assets and gain indirect control over Kanpur Fertilizers and Chemicals Limited. The move aligns with APSEZ's aggressive inorganic growth in the infrastructure sector.

item.title: Welspun Corp Buys 45% WCSL Stake for ₹45,000; Lifts RE Power Payment to ₹75 Cr
item.description: Welspun Corp's board has approved acquiring a 45% stake in group entity Welspun Corporate Services Limited (WCSL) for a nominal ₹45,000. WCSL will become an associate company, serving as a centralized corporate services platform for the Welspun Group. The company also revised its payment for renewable energy power transmission rights for its Anjar facility upwards to ₹75 Crores, from the previously approved ₹35 Crores.

item.title: Motilal Oswal Shuts Trading Window for FY26 Results Announcement
item.description: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Limited (MOFSL) has announced the closure of its trading window effective April 1, 2026. This temporary restriction is to facilitate the approval of the audited financial results for the quarter and fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. Designated persons, including directors and promoters, are prohibited from trading company shares during this period to prevent potential insider trading.

item.title: GIFT City Faces IFSCA Crackdown to Boost Global Hub Credibility
item.description: The International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) has significantly ramped up enforcement against Fund Management Entities (FMEs) and Capital Market Intermediaries (CMIs) in Gujarat's GIFT City. Approximately 10 entities have received show-cause notices for alleged lapses in "substance requirements," operational deficiencies, and compliance failures. This regulatory tightening, while potentially creating friction, underscores GIFT City's strategy to establish itself as a credible, substance-driven financial hub aligned with international standards, rather than merely a low-tax jurisdiction.

item.title: NHIT Sets ₹149.50 Floor for Unit Sale: Bidding March 20-23
item.description: National Highways Trust (NHIT) has approved an institutional placement of units, setting a floor price of ₹149.50. Bidding for these units will take place from March 20 to March 23, 2026. The funds raised will support NHIT's acquisition of new infrastructure assets and portfolio expansion.

item.title: Wardwizard Healthcare Board Meeting: Approve Deals, Auditors Amid Key Exits
item.description: Wardwizard Healthcare Limited is set to hold a board meeting on March 23, 2026. Key agenda items include approving material related party transactions and appointing auditors for FY 2026-27. The meeting will also acknowledge the resignations of Director Ms. Sheetal Mandar Bhalerao and CFO Ms. Mittalben Patel, and consider Mr. Yuvraj Priyadarshi for Additional Director, subject to shareholder approval.

item.title: Coromandel International: Algavista Greentech Moves From Promoter Group
item.description: Coromandel International has received approvals from the NSE and BSE to reclassify Algavista Greentech Private Limited. Algavista moves from the company's promoter group to public shareholder status, aligning with SEBI rules following a change in its own shareholding.

item.title: India's UK/EU Trade Deals Hit by Geopolitical Risk
item.description: India's ambitious push for Free Trade Agreements with the UK and EU in 2026 faces significant hurdles. While the UK deal is set for near-term implementation, the EU pact's ratification extends into 2027. Simultaneously, escalating geopolitical tensions in West Asia are driving up oil prices, destabilizing currency, and widening India's trade deficit, creating a precarious balance between trade opportunity and economic risk.

item.title: India's Domestic Demand Shields Economy from Oil Price Shock
item.description: India's economy is strong, driven by domestic spending and investment, which helps it withstand global shocks like high oil prices. While the economy's core health is good, prolonged Middle East conflicts could raise inflation, widen the trade gap, and affect energy supplies. Growth forecasts for FY26 remain positive, but depend on a stable global situation.

item.title: SEBI Sees FPI Opportunity Amid $9B Outflow; Encourages Russian Investors
item.description: SEBI official Kamlesh Chandra Varshney sees a 'tremendous opportunity' for foreign investors in Indian equities amid market corrections. However, this positive outlook clashes with substantial FPI outflows exceeding ₹77,000 crore by mid-March 2026. These outflows stem from Middle East tensions, rising oil prices, and a weaker rupee, hitting the financial sector hard.

item.title: Saraswati Commercial Secures Bharti Airtel Shares After ₹23.56 Cr Payment
item.description: Saraswati Commercial (India) Ltd. has finalized its ₹23.56 crore payment to convert 5,87,212 partly paid-up Bharti Airtel shares into fully paid-up ones. This move solidifies the NBFC's investment, though the shares are pending final listing approvals.

item.title: Sumuka Agro to Buy Arkaa Cluster for ₹35 Crore in Share Swap
item.description: Sumuka Agro Industries Limited has signed a binding term sheet to acquire Arkaa Cluster Private Limited for ₹35 crore through a share swap. Arkaa Cluster, involved in food and agro commodity trading with a FY25 turnover of ₹150.61 crore, will fuel Sumuka Agro's strategic diversification into complementary segments. The deal aims to enhance synergies and expand its presence.

item.title: Anil Ambani Questioned by CBI in ₹2,929 Cr RCom Loan Fraud Case
item.description: Reliance Group Chairperson Anil Ambani was interrogated for eight hours by the CBI regarding an alleged ₹2,929 crore loan fraud by Reliance Communications (RCom). The investigation stems from an SBI complaint, which classified RCom's accounts as fraudulent last year. Authum Investment, which acquired RCom assets, also saw its director questioned. This adds significant pressure to RCom's ongoing insolvency proceedings and follows separate fraud allegations involving Anil Ambani's son, Jai Anmol Ambani.

item.title: Firstsource Solutions Launches AI Model Focused on Outcome Contracts
item.description: Firstsource Solutions has launched 'Intelligence That Operates', a new AI-driven operating model. This agent-first approach aims to deliver underwritten outcomes for enterprises, shifting from effort-based contracts to revenue tied to results. It emphasizes five core principles: Domain Intelligence, Full-Stack Delivery, Compounding Intelligence, Outcome Accountability, and Governed Autonomy.

item.title: TIIT offers ₹400 Cr guarantee for subsidiary's NSE margin needs
item.description: The Investment Trust of India Limited (TIIT) has issued a ₹400 crore corporate guarantee to HDFC Bank. This secures a bank guarantee for its wholly-owned subsidiary, Antique Stock Broking Limited (ASBL), to meet collateral and margin requirements with NSE Clearing. TIIT has stated the transaction is in the ordinary course of business.

item.title: Gujarat Kidney Profit Jumps 10% H1 FY26; Appoints Auditors Amid Compliance Focus
item.description: Gujarat Kidney And Superspeciality Limited appointed Mr. Dharmendra Bhaliya as Secretarial Auditor and Mr. Siddharth Atulbhai Shah as Internal Auditor for FY25-26. The company reported its standalone financial results for the half-year ended September 30, 2025, showing a 12.97% rise in revenue to ₹21.09 crore and a 10.12% increase in profit after tax to ₹6.20 crore compared to the prior year period.
